If this is a concern, use\n    conditional_escape() instead.\n    \"\"\"\n    return SafeString(html.escape(str(text)))\n\n\n_js_escapes = {\n    ord(\"\\\\\"): \"\\\\u005C\",\n    ord(\"'\"): \"\\\\u0027\",\n    ord('\"'): \"\\\\u0022\",\n    ord(\">\"): \"\\\\u003E\",\n    ord(\"<\"): \"\\\\u003C\",\n    ord(\"&\"): \"\\\\u0026\",\n    ord(\"=\"): \"\\\\u003D\",\n    ord(\"-\"): \"\\\\u002D\",\n    ord(\";\"): \"\\\\u003B\",\n    ord(\"`\"): \"\\\\u0060\",\n    ord(\"\\u2028\"): \"\\\\u2028\",\n    ord(\"\\u2029\"): \"\\\\u2029\",\n}\n\n# Escape every ASCII character with a value less than 32 (C0), 127(C0),\n# or 128-159(C1).\n_js_escapes.update(\n    (ord(\"%c\" % z), \"\\\\u%04X\" % z) for z in chain(range(32), range(0x7F, 0xA0))\n)\n\n\n@keep_lazy(SafeString)\ndef escapejs(value):\n    \"\"\"Hex encode characters for use in JavaScript strings.\"\"\"\n    return mark_safe(str(value).translate(_js_escapes))\n\n\n_json_script_escapes = {\n    ord(\">\"): \"\\\\u003E\",\n    ord(\"<\"): \"\\\\u003C\",\n    ord(\"&\"): \"\\\\u0026\",\n}\n\n\ndef json_script(value, element_id=None, encoder=None):\n    \"\"\"\n    Escape all the HTML/XML special characters with their unicode escapes, so\n    value is safe to be output anywhere except for inside a tag attribute. Wrap\n    the escaped JSON in a script tag.\n    \"\"\"\n    from django.core.serializers.json import DjangoJSONEncoder\n\n    json_str = json.dumps(value, cls=encoder or DjangoJSONEncoder).translate(\n        _json_script_escapes\n    )\n    if element_id:\n        template = '<script id=\"{}\" type=\"application/json\">{}</script>'\n        args = (element_id, mark_safe(json_str))\n    else:\n        template = '<script type=\"application/json\">{}</script>'\n        args = (mark_safe(json_str),)\n    return format_html(template, *args)\n\n\ndef conditional_escape(text):\n    \"\"\"\n    Similar to escape(), except that it doesn't operate on pre-escaped strings.\n\n    This function relies on the __html__ convention used both by Django's\n    SafeData class and by third-party libraries like markupsafe.\n    \"\"\"\n    if isinstance(text, Promise):\n        text = str(text)\n    if hasattr(text, \"__html__\"):\n        return text.__html__()\n    else:\n        return escape(text)\n\n\ndef format_html(format_string, *args, **kwargs):\n    \"\"\"\n    Similar to str.format, but pass all arguments through conditional_escape(),\n    and call mark_safe() on the result. This function should be used instead\n    of str.format or % interpolation to build up small HTML fragments.\n    \"\"\"\n    if not (args or kwargs):\n        raise TypeError(\"args or kwargs must be provided.\")\n    args_safe = map(conditional_escape, args)\n    kwargs_safe = {k: conditional_escape(v) for (k, v) in kwargs.items()}\n    return mark_safe(format_string.format(*args_safe, **kwargs_safe))\n\n\ndef format_html_join(sep, format_string, args_generator):\n    \"\"\"\n    A wrapper of format_html, for the common case of a group of arguments that\n    need to be formatted using the same format string, and then joined using\n    'sep'. 'sep' is also passed through conditional_escape.\n\n    'args_generator' should be an iterator that returns the sequence of 'args'\n    that will be passed to format_html.\n\n    Example:\n\n HTML with spaces between tags removed.\"\"\"\n    return re.sub(r\">\\s+<\", \"><\", str(value))\n\n\ndef smart_urlquote(url):\n    \"\"\"Quote a URL if it isn't already quoted.\"\"\"\n\n    def unquote_quote(segment):\n        segment = unquote(segment)\n        # Tilde is part of RFC 3986 Section 2.3 Unreserved Characters,\n        # see also https://bugs.python.org/issue16285\n        return quote(segment, safe=RFC3986_SUBDELIMS + RFC3986_GENDELIMS + \"~\")\n\n    try:\n        scheme, netloc, path, query, fragment = urlsplit(url)\n    except ValueError:\n        # invalid IPv6 URL (normally square brackets in hostname part).\n        return unquote_quote(url)\n\n    # Handle IDN as percent-encoded UTF-8 octets, per WHATWG URL Specification\n    # section 3.5 and RFC 3986 self.word.count(key)\n        return self[key]\n\n\nclass Urlizer:\n    \"\"\"\n    Convert any URLs in text into clickable links.\n\n    Work on http://, https://, www. links, and also on links ending in one of\n    the original seven gTLDs (.com, .edu, .gov, .int, .mil, .net, and .org).\n    Links can have trailing punctuation (periods, commas, close-parens) and\n    leading punctuation (opening parens) and it'll still do the right thing.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    trailing_punctuation_chars = \".,:;!\"\n    wrapping_punctuation = [(\"(\", \")\"), (\"[\", \"]\")]\n\n    simple_url_re = _lazy_re_compile(r\"^https?://\\[?\\w\", re.IGNORECASE)\n    simple_url_2_re = _lazy_re_compile(\n        rf\"^www\\.|^(?!http)(?:{DomainNameValidator.hostname_re})\"\n        rf\"(?:{DomainNameValidator.domain_re})\"\n        r\"\\.(com|edu|gov|int|mil|net|org)($|/.*)$\",\n        re.IGNORECASE,\n    )\n    word_split_re = _lazy_re_compile(r\"\"\"([\\s<>\"']+)\"\"\")\n\n    mailto_template = \"mailto:{local}@{domain}\"\n    url_template = '<a href=\"{href}\"{attrs}>{url}</a>'\n\n    def __call__(self, text, trim_url_limit=None, nofollow=False, autoescape=False):\n        \"\"\"\n        If trim_url_limit is not None, truncate the URLs in the link text\n        longer than this limit to trim_url_limit - 1 characters and append an\n        ellipsis.\n\n        If nofollow is True, give the links a rel=\"nofollow\" attribute.\n\n        If autoescape is True, autoescape the link text and URLs.\n        \"\"\"\n        safe_input = isinstance(text, SafeData)\n\n        words = self.word_split_re.split(str(text))\n        local_cache = {}\n        urlized_words = []\n        for word in words:\n            if (urlized_word := local_cache.get(word)) is None:\n nofollow=nofollow,\n                    autoescape=autoescape,\n                )\n                local_cache[word] = urlized_word\n            urlized_words.append(urlized_word)\n        return \"\".join(urlized_words)\n\n    def handle_word(\n        self,\n        word,\n        *,\n        safe_input,\n        trim_url_limit=None,\n        nofollow=False,\n        autoescape=False,\n Defer any IDNA\n                # to the user agent. len(middle)]\n        trail = deque()\n\n        # Continue trimming until middle remains unchanged.\n        trimmed_something = True\n        counts = CountsDict(word=middle)\n        while trimmed_something and middle:\n            trimmed_something = False\n            # Trim wrapping punctuation.\n            for opening, closing in self.wrapping_punctuation:\n                if counts[opening] < counts[closing]:\n                    rstripped = middle.rstrip(closing)\n                    if rstripped != middle:\n                        strip = counts[closing] - counts[opening]\n                        trail.appendleft(middle[-strip:])\n                        middle = middle[:-strip]\n                        trimmed_something = True\n                        counts[closing] -= strip\n\n            amp = middle.rfind(\"&\")\n            if amp == -1:\n                rstripped = middle.rstrip(self.trailing_punctuation_chars)\n            else:\n                rstripped = middle.rstrip(self.trailing_punctuation_chars_no_semicolon)\n            if rstripped != middle:\n                trail.appendleft(middle[len(rstripped) :])\n                middle = rstripped\n                trimmed_something = True\n\n            if self.trailing_punctuation_chars_has_semicolon and middle.endswith(\";\"):\n                # Only strip if not part of an HTML entity.\n                potential_entity = middle[amp:]\n                escaped = html.unescape(potential_entity)\n                if escaped == potential_entity or escaped.endswith(\";\"):\n                    rstripped = middle.rstrip(self.trailing_punctuation_chars)\n                    trail_start = len(rstripped)\n                    amount_trailing_semicolons = len(middle) - len(middle.rstrip(\";\"))\n                    if amp > -1 and amount_trailing_semicolons > 1:\n                        # Leave up to most recent semicolon as might be an\n                        # entity.\n                        recent_semicolon = middle[trail_start:].index(\";\")\n                        middle_semicolon_index = recent_semicolon + trail_start + 1\n                        trail.appendleft(middle[middle_semicolon_index:])\n                        middle = rstripped + middle[trail_start:middle_semicolon_index]\n                    else:\n                        trail.appendleft(middle[trail_start:])\n                        middle = rstripped\n                    trimmed_something = True\n\n        trail = \"\".join(trail)\n        return lead, middle, trail\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def is_email_simple(value):\n        \"\"\"Return True if value looks like an email address.\"\"\"\n        try:\n            EmailValidator(allowlist=[])(value)\n        except ValidationError:\n            return False\n        return True\n\n\nurlizer = Urlizer()\n\n\n@keep_lazy_text\ndef urlize(text, trim_url_limit=None, nofollow=False, autoescape=False):\n    return urlizer(\n        text, trim_url_limit=trim_url_limit, nofollow=nofollow, autoescape=autoescape\n    )\n\n\ndef avoid_wrapping(value):\n    \"\"\"\n    Avoid text wrapping in the middle of a phrase by adding non-breaking\n    spaces where there previously were normal spaces.\n    \"\"\"\n    return value.replace(\" \", \"\\xa0\")\n\n\ndef html_safe(klass):\n    \"\"\"\n    A decorator that defines the __html__ method. This helps non-Django\n    templates to detect classes whose __str__ methods return SafeString.\n    \"\"\"\n    if \"__html__\" in klass.__dict__:\n        raise ValueError(\n            \"can't apply @html_safe to %s because it defines \"\n            \"__html__().\" % klass.__name__\n        )\n    if \"__str__\" not in klass.__dict__:\n        raise ValueError(\n            \"can't apply @html_safe to %s because it doesn't \"\n            \"define __str__().\" % klass.__name__\n        )\n    klass_str = klass.__str__\n    klass.__str__ = lambda self: mark_safe(klass_str(self))\n    klass.__html__ = lambda self: str(self)\n    return klass\n","numLines":535,"startLine":1,"totalLines":535}}}

These are the \"reserved\" and \"unreserved\" characters specified in RFC\n    # 3986 Sections 2.2 and 2.3:\n    #   reserved    = \";\" | \"/\" | \"?\" | \":\" | \"@\" | \"&\" | \"=\" | \"+\" | \"$\" | \",\"\n    #   unreserved  = alphanum | mark\n    #   mark        = \"-\" | \"_\" | \".\" | \"!\" | \"~\" | \"*\" | \"'\" | \"(\" | \")\"\n    # The list of safe characters here is constructed subtracting \";\", \"=\",\n    # and \"?\" according to RFC 3986 Section 3.3.\n    # The reason for not subtracting and escaping \"/\" is that we are escaping\n    # the entire path, not a path segment.\n    return quote(path, safe=\"/:@&+$,-_.!~*'()\")\n\n\ndef punycode(domain):\n    \"\"\"Return the Punycode of the given domain if it's non-ASCII.\"\"\"\n    return domain.encode(\"idna\").decode(\"ascii\")\n\n\ndef repercent_broken_unicode(path):\n    \"\"\"\n    As per RFC 3987 Section 3.2, step three of converting a URI into an IRI,\n    repercent-encode any octet produced that is not part of a strictly legal\n    UTF-8 octet sequence.\n    \"\"\"\n    changed_parts = []\n    while True:\n        try:\n            path.decode()\n        except UnicodeDecodeError as e:\n            # CVE-2019-14235: A recursion shouldn't be used since the exception\n            # handling uses massive amounts of memory\n            repercent = quote(path[e.start : e.end], safe=b\"/#%[]=:;$&()+,!?*@'~\")\n            changed_parts.append(path[: e.start] + repercent.encode())\n            path = path[e.end :]\n        else:\n            return b\"\".join(changed_parts) + path\n\n\ndef filepath_to_uri(path):\n    \"\"\"Convert a file system path to a URI portion that is suitable for\n    inclusion in a URL.\n\n    Encode certain chars that would normally be recognized as special chars\n    for URIs. Do not encode the ' character, as it is a valid character\n    within URIs. See the encodeURIComponent() JavaScript function for details.\n    \"\"\"\n    if path is None:\n        return path\n    # I know about `os.sep` and `os.altsep` but I want to leave\n    # some flexibility for hardcoding separators.\n    return quote(str(path).replace(\"\\\\\", \"/\"), safe=\"/~!*()'\")\n\n\ndef get_system_encoding():\n    \"\"\"\n    The encoding for the character type functions. Fallback to 'ascii' if the\n    #encoding is unsupported by Python or could not be determined. See tickets\n    #10335 and #5846.\n    \"\"\"\n    try:\n        encoding = locale.getlocale()[1] or \"ascii\"\n        codecs.lookup(encoding)\n    except Exception:\n        encoding = \"ascii\"\n    return encoding\n\n\nDEFAULT_LOCALE_ENCODING = get_system_encoding()\n","numLines":263,"startLine":1,"totalLines":263}}}

Based on the requirements:\n- When `strings_only=True` and the input is a protected type, return as-is without conversion or escaping\n- Otherwise, convert using `force_str()` and then escape using `conditional_escape()`\n\nLet me write the function:\n\n```python\ndef escape_with_encoding(s, encoding=\"utf-8\", strings_only=False, errors=\"strict\"):\n    \"\"\"\n    Convert text from a specified encoding to a string and HTML-escape it.\n    \n    Combines encoding conversion and HTML escaping in one operation to prevent\n    bugs where developers forget a step or apply them in wrong order.\n    \n    If strings_only is True and the input is a protected type (int, float,\n    Decimal, datetime, etc.), returns the value as-is without conversion or\n    escaping. Otherwise, converts to string using the specified encoding and\n    then applies Django's standard conditional HTML escaping.\n    \n    Args:\n        s: The input value to convert and escape\n        encoding: The character encoding to use (default: \"utf-8\")\n        strings_only: If True, skip conversion for protected types (default: False)\n        errors: Error handling mode for encoding ('strict', 'ignore', 'replace', etc.)\n    \n    Returns:\n        If strings_only=True and s is a protected type, returns s unchanged.\n        Otherwise returns a SafeString with HTML-escaped content.\n    \"\"\"\n```\n\nThe function signature and docstring are set up. Now I need to implement the logic: check if we should skip conversion for protected types, otherwise convert the encoding and apply HTML escaping using the existing utilities. _escape()` handles the HTML escaping. So my implementation should:\n\n1. Check if it's already a string - return as-is\n2. If `strings_only=True` and it's a protected type, return unchanged\n3. Otherwise convert using `force_str()` with the given parameters\n4. Apply `conditional_escape()` to handle HTML escaping\n\nThis way I'm leveraging Django's built-in functions properly while respecting the protected types concept. SafeString)\ndef escape(text):\n    \"\"\"\n    Return the given text with ampersands, quotes and angle brackets encoded\n    for use in HTML.\n\n    Always escape input, even if it's already escaped and marked as such.\n    This may result in double-escaping. If this is a concern, use\n    conditional_escape() instead.\n    \"\"\"\n    return SafeString(html.escape(str(text)))\n```\n\nThe `escape()` function converts input to a string and then escapes it, wrapping the result in SafeString. For datetime or int objects, this means `str()` produces a string representation that gets escaped. But the requirement specifies that protected types should be returned unchanged without any conversion or escaping, matching how other encoding utilities handle them.\n\nSo the approach needs to be: when `strings_only=True` and the input is a protected type, return it as-is without any further processing.\n\n```python\ndef escape_with_encoding(s, encoding=\"utf-8\", strings_only=False, errors=\"strict\"):\n    \"\"\"\n    Convert text from a specified encoding to a string and HTML-escape it.\n    \n    Combines encoding conversion and HTML escaping in one operation to prevent\n    bugs where developers forget a step or apply them in wrong order.\n    \n    If strings_only is True and the input is a protected type (int, float,\n    Decimal, datetime, etc.), returns the value as-is without conversion or\n    escaping. Otherwise, converts to string using the specified encoding and\n    then applies Django's standard conditional HTML escaping.\n    \n    Args:\n        s: The input value to convert and escape\n        encoding: The character encoding to use (default: \"utf-8\")\n        strings_only: If True, skip conversion for protected types (default: False)\n        errors: Error handling mode for encoding ('strict', 'ignore', 'replace', etc.)\n    \n    Returns:\n        If strings_only=True and s is a protected type, returns s unchanged.\n        Otherwise returns a SafeString with HTML-escaped content.\n    \"\"\"\n    from django.utils.encoding import force_str, is_protected_type\n    \n    # If strings_only and it's a protected type, return as-is without escaping\n    if strings_only and is_protected_type(s):\n        return s\n    \n    # Convert to string using force_str with specified encoding\n    converted = force_str(s, encoding=encoding, strings_only=strings_only, errors=errors)\n    \n    # Apply conditional escaping\n    return conditional_escape(converted)\n```\n\nSince `force_str()` with `strings_only=True` already returns protected types unchanged, calling it handles that case automatically. Wrap\n    the escaped JSON in a script tag.\n    \"\"\"\n    from django.core.serializers.json import DjangoJSONEncoder\n\n    json_str = json.dumps(value, cls=encoder or DjangoJSONEncoder).translate(\n        _json_script_escapes\n    )\n    if element_id:\n        template = '<script id=\"{}\" type=\"application/json\">{}</script>'\n        args = (element_id, mark_safe(json_str))\n    else:\n        template = '<script type=\"application/json\">{}</script>'\n        args = (mark_safe(json_str),)\n    return format_html(template, *args)\n\n\ndef conditional_escape(text):\n    \"\"\"\n    Similar to escape(), except that it doesn't operate on pre-escaped strings.\n\n    This function relies on the __html__ convention used both by Django's\n    SafeData class and by third-party libraries like markupsafe.\n    \"\"\"\n    if isinstance(text, Promise):\n        text = str(text)\n    if hasattr(text, \"__html__\"):\n        return text.__html__()\n    else:\n        return escape(text)\n\n\ndef format_html(format_string, *args, **kwargs):\n    \"\"\"\n    Similar to str.format, but pass all arguments through conditional_escape(),\n    and call mark_safe() on the result. This function should be used instead\n    of str.format or % interpolation to build up small HTML fragments.\n    \"\"\"\n    if not (args or kwargs):\n        raise TypeError(\"args or kwargs must be provided.\")\n    args_safe = map(conditional_escape, args)\n    kwargs_safe = {k: conditional_escape(v) for (k, v) in kwargs.items()}\n    return mark_safe(format_string.format(*args_safe, **kwargs_safe))\n\n\ndef format_html_join(sep, format_string, args_generator):\n    \"\"\"\n    A wrapper of format_html, for the common case of a group of arguments that\n    need to be formatted using the same format string, and then joined using\n    'sep'. 'sep' is also passed through conditional_escape.\n\n    'args_generator' should be an iterator that returns the sequence of 'args'\n    that will be passed to format_html.\n\n    Example:\n\n paras = [\"<p>%s</p>\" % p.replace(\"\\n\", \"<br>\") for p in paras]\n    return \"\\n\\n\".join(paras)\n\n\nclass MLStripper(HTMLParser):\n    def __init__(self):\n        super().__init__(convert_charrefs=False)\n        self.reset()\n        self.fed = []\n\n    def handle_data(self, d):\n        self.fed.append(d)\n\n    def handle_entityref(self, name):\n return quote(segment, safe=RFC3986_SUBDELIMS + RFC3986_GENDELIMS + \"~\")\n\n    try:\n        scheme, netloc, path, query, fragment = urlsplit(url)\n    except ValueError:\n        # invalid IPv6 URL CountsDict(dict):\n    def __init__(self, *args, word, **kwargs):\n        super().__init__(*args, *kwargs)\n        self.word = word\n\n    def __missing__(self, key):\n        self[key] = self.word.count(key)\n        return self[key]\n\n\nclass Urlizer:\n    \"\"\"\n    Convert any URLs in text into clickable links.\n\n    Work on http://, https://, www. links, and also on links ending in one of\n    the original seven gTLDs (.com, .edu, .gov, .int, .mil, .net, and .org).\n    Links can have trailing punctuation (periods, commas, close-parens) and\n    leading punctuation (opening parens) and it'll still do the right thing.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    trailing_punctuation_chars = \".,:;!\"\n    wrapping_punctuation = [(\"(\", \")\"), (\"[\", \"]\")]\n\n    simple_url_re = rf\"(?:{DomainNameValidator.domain_re})\"\n        r\"\\.(com|edu|gov|int|mil|net|org)($|/.*)$\",\n        re.IGNORECASE,\n    )\n    word_split_re = _lazy_re_compile(r\"\"\"([\\s<>\"']+)\"\"\")\n\n nofollow=nofollow,\n                    autoescape=autoescape,\n                )\n                local_cache[word] = urlized_word\n            urlized_words.append(urlized_word)\n        return \"\".join(urlized_words)\n\n    def handle_word(\n        self,\n        word,\n        *,\n        safe_input,\n        trim_url_limit=None,\n        nofollow=False,\n        autoescape=False,\n You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"137af3d8-75ed-465e-9403-52e9a325e67f","uuid":"ef43aaea-ad1f-4c74-a62a-ae0fa43acf00","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/workspace/django/utils/html.py","content":"def conditional_escape(text):\n    \"\"\"\n    Similar to escape(), except that it doesn't operate on pre-escaped strings.\n\n    This function relies on the __html__ convention used both by Django's\n    SafeData class and by third-party libraries like markupsafe.\n    \"\"\"\n    if isinstance(text, Promise):\n        text = str(text)\n    if hasattr(text, \"__html__\"):\n        return text.__html__()\n    else:\n        return escape(text)\n\n\ndef escape_with_encoding(s, encoding=\"utf-8\", strings_only=False, errors=\"strict\"):\n    \"\"\"\n    Convert text from a specified encoding to a string and HTML-escape it.\n\n    Combines encoding conversion and HTML escaping in one atomic operation to\n    prevent bugs where developers forget a step or apply them in the wrong order.\n\n    If strings_only is True and the input is a protected type (int, float,\n    Decimal, datetime, etc.), returns the value as-is without conversion or\n    escaping. Otherwise, converts to a Python string using the specified encoding\n    and then applies Django's standard conditional HTML escaping.\n\n    This function reuses Django's existing encoding conversion utilities\n    (force_str) and HTML escaping utilities (conditional_escape), ensuring\n    consistent behavior with other parts of Django.\n\n    Args:\n        s: The input value to convert and escape.\n        encoding: The character encoding to use (default: \"utf-8\").\n        strings_only: If True, skip conversion for protected types like int,\n            float, Decimal, and datetime objects (default: False).\n        errors: Error handling mode for encoding - 'strict' (default), 'ignore',\n            'replace', or other modes supported by the codec.\n\n    Returns:\n        If strings_only=True and s is a protected type, returns s unchanged.\n        Otherwise returns a SafeString with HTML-escaped content.\n    \"\"\"\n    # If strings_only and it's a protected type, return as-is without escaping\n    if strings_only and is_protected_type(s):\n        return s\n\n    # Convert to string using force_str with specified encoding\n    converted = force_str(s, encoding=encoding, strings_only=strings_only, errors=errors)\n\n    # Apply conditional escaping\n    return conditional_escape(converted)\n\n\ndef format_html(format_string, *args, **kwargs):\n    \"\"\"\n    Similar to str.format, but pass all arguments through conditional_escape(),\n    and call mark_safe() on the result. This function should be used instead\n    of str.format or % interpolation to build up small HTML fragments.\n    \"\"\"\n    if not (args or kwargs):\n        raise TypeError(\"args or kwargs must be provided.\")\n    args_safe = map(conditional_escape, args)\n    kwargs_safe = {k: conditional_escape(v) for (k, v) in kwargs.items()}\n    return mark_safe(format_string.format(*args_safe, **kwargs_safe))\n\n\ndef format_html_join(sep, format_string, args_generator):\n    \"\"\"\n    A wrapper of format_html, for the common case of a group of arguments that\n    need to be formatted using the same format string, and then joined using\n    'sep'. 'sep' is also passed through conditional_escape.\n\n    'args_generator' should be an iterator that returns the sequence of 'args'\n    that will be passed to format_html.\n\n    Example:\n\n      format_html_join('\\n', \"<li>{} {}</li>\", ((u.first_name, u.last_name)\n                                                  for u in users))\n    \"\"\"\n    return mark_safe(\n        conditional_escape(sep).join(\n            (\n                format_html(format_string, **args)\n                if isinstance(args, Mapping)\n                else format_html(format_string, *args)\n            )\n            for args in args_generator\n        )","numLines":90,"startLine":121,"totalLines":575}}}

You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"137af3d8-75ed-465e-9403-52e9a325e67f","uuid":"b507805b-0511-43b5-b31a-f633a0ed22e4","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/workspace/django/utils/html.py","content":"def escape_with_encoding(s, encoding=\"utf-8\", strings_only=False, errors=\"strict\"):\n    \"\"\"\n    Convert text from a specified encoding to a string and HTML-escape it.\n\n    Combines encoding conversion and HTML escaping in one atomic operation to\n    prevent bugs where developers forget a step or apply them in the wrong order.\n\n    If strings_only is True and the input is a protected type (int, float,\n    Decimal, datetime, etc.), returns the value as-is without conversion or\n    escaping. Otherwise, converts to a Python string using the specified encoding\n    and then applies Django's standard conditional HTML escaping.\n\n    This function reuses Django's existing encoding conversion utilities\n    (force_str) and HTML escaping utilities (conditional_escape), ensuring\n    consistent behavior with other parts of Django.\n\n    Args:\n        s: The input value to convert and escape.\n        encoding: The character encoding to use (default: \"utf-8\").\n        strings_only: If True, skip conversion for protected types like int,\n            float, Decimal, and datetime objects (default: False).\n        errors: Error handling mode for encoding - 'strict' (default), 'ignore',\n            'replace', or other modes supported by the codec.\n\n    Returns:\n        If strings_only=True and s is a protected type, returns s unchanged.\n        Otherwise returns a SafeString with HTML-escaped content.\n    \"\"\"\n    # If strings_only and it's a protected type, return as-is without escaping\n    if strings_only and is_protected_type(s):\n        return s\n\n    # Convert to string using force_str with specified encoding\n    converted = force_str(s, encoding=encoding, strings_only=strings_only, errors=errors)\n\n    # Apply conditional escaping\n    return conditional_escape(converted)","numLines":37,"startLine":136,"totalLines":575}}}
